title: adactylus nudus
side_effects: Adactylus nudus can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, including skin irritation, itching, and swelling. Ingesting the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as interact with certain medications. Long-term exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can cause liver damage and potentially lead to kidney failure. Improper use of Adactylus nudus can also trigger respiratory issues, including asthma-like symptoms and difficulty breathing.
